directive: ROOT NOVELTY REFRAME: Treat the Hölder/fractional-color Bernstein exposure-HT interval as an established cited preliminary corollary of Pelekis--Ramon--Wang (2017) plus Aronow--Samii (2017), not as the novel theorem or headline. Make the sole field-tier kernel a fully quantified minimax confidence-length lower bound/frontier on an explicit graph family whose exposure experiment cannot be compressed to Sandoval et al.'s ordinary rare-treatment/block experiment. Specify the graph family, exposure mapping, Bernoulli design, propensity regime, fixed potential-outcome class, uniformly valid interval class, least-favorable two-schedule construction, and exact Hellinger/Le Cam target; explain concretely why overlapping neighborhoods create information geometry absent from a single common block coordinate. If that non-compressible frontier cannot be stated honestly, pivot to the strongest genuinely distinct graph-dependent lower-bound target rather than re-claiming novelty for the standard upper bound. SYNC ALL PROSE FIELDS and positioning (tldr, gap/niche/fill, related_work, introduction, results-at-a-glance) so the upper interval is supporting machinery and no text calls it first/new; distinguish Sandoval et al., Pelekis--Ramon--Wang, Aronow--Samii, Tchetgen Tchetgen--VanderWeele, and Rigdon--Hudgens wholesale.
